message:
test(update-channel): align fixtures with current update.sh resolver API

scripts/tests/test_update_channel.sh was still mocking helpers from
a prior generation of scripts/lib/update.sh. After the recent round
of hardening work — passwd-based home resolution, pinned binary
paths, the TARGET_HOME propagation chain, and the dry-run
non-mutation fixes — several of the names the tests intercepted no
longer exist, and a couple of fixtures were relying on paths that
aren't guaranteed to be present on every runner (notably /srv/dummy).

Adjustments (all test-only; no production code touched):

- "Test 2b: update_target_home resolves passwd homes": stop
  overriding the system getent() entirely, which is both heavier
  than needed and fragile on hosts that have no passwd entry for
  the test user. Switch to stubbing the narrower
  update_getent_passwd_entry() helper that update.sh actually
  calls, and point it at a real temp directory created via mktemp
  ($TMPDIR/acfs-home-resolution.$$) so the assertion checks for a
  path that genuinely exists.
- "Test 3b: Gemini dry-run skips nvm warnings cleanly" and
  "Test 3e: legacy cleanup stays non-destructive in dry-run":
  re-order the bash -c body so HOME and TARGET_HOME are both set
  before UPDATE_SH is sourced. Previously UPDATE_SH was sourced
  first and then HOME was overwritten, which defeated the new
  fail-closed-on-unresolved-TARGET_HOME behavior introduced in
  commit 958e2ee2. Rename cmd_exists() stubs to
  update_binary_exists() to match the renamed helper from the
  "migrate all shell libraries to the new passwd/user resolvers"
  refactor.
- log_item() stub: make the 3rd positional optional ("${3:-}")
  so callers that don't pass a detail string don't blow up under
  set -u, matching how production log_item handles its arguments.
- Add update_binary_path() and update_run_in_target_context()
  stubs inside the ms_arm64_arch loop so the verified-installer
  harness exercises the same resolver surface the real code now
  goes through (printing "cargo" as the resolved path, and
  running commands inline instead of re-entering a target
  context).

Net effect: the update-channel test now runs cleanly against the
post-refactor update.sh without silently passing on hosts that
happened to have a matching /srv layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -90,25 +90,28 @@ fi
 # ============================================================
 section "Test 2b: update_target_home resolves passwd homes"
 # ============================================================
+home_resolution_dir="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/acfs-home-resolution.$$"
+mkdir -p "$home_resolution_dir"
 home_resolution_output=$(
+    HOME_RESOLUTION_DIR="$home_resolution_dir" \
     bash -c '
         source "'"$UPDATE_SH"'"
         TARGET_HOME=""
         HOME="/tmp/not-the-target-home"
 
-        getent() {
-            if [[ "$1" == "passwd" && "$2" == "dummy" ]]; then
-                printf "dummy:x:1000:1000::/srv/dummy:/bin/bash\n"
+        update_getent_passwd_entry() {
+            if [[ "$1" == "dummy" ]]; then
+                printf "dummy:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n" "$HOME_RESOLUTION_DIR"
                 return 0
             fi
-            command getent "$@"
+            return 1
         }
 
         update_target_home dummy
     ' 2>&1
 ) || true
 
-if [[ "$home_resolution_output" == "/srv/dummy" ]]; then
+if [[ "$home_resolution_output" == "$home_resolution_dir" ]]; then
     pass "update_target_home prefers passwd-resolved homes over /home fallback"
 else
     fail "update_target_home did not use passwd-resolved home: $home_resolution_output"
@@ -157,14 +160,15 @@ section "Test 3b: Gemini dry-run skips nvm warnings cleanly"
 # ============================================================
 gemini_dry_run_output=$(
     bash -c '
-        source "'"$UPDATE_SH"'"
-
         temp_home="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/acfs-gemini-dry-run.$$"
         mkdir -p "$temp_home/.bun/bin"
         printf "#!/usr/bin/env bash\nexit 0\n" > "$temp_home/.bun/bin/bun"
         chmod +x "$temp_home/.bun/bin/bun"
 
         HOME="$temp_home"
+        TARGET_HOME="$temp_home"
+        source "'"$UPDATE_SH"'"
+
         DRY_RUN=true
         VERBOSE=false
         QUIET=true
@@ -182,8 +186,8 @@ gemini_dry_run_output=$(
         declare -gA VERSION_BEFORE=()
         declare -gA VERSION_AFTER=()
 
-        log_item() { printf "%s|%s|%s\n" "$1" "$2" "$3"; }
-        cmd_exists() { [[ "$1" == "gemini" ]]; }
+        log_item() { printf "%s|%s|%s\n" "$1" "$2" "${3:-}"; }
+        update_binary_exists() { [[ "$1" == "gemini" ]]; }
         capture_version_before() { return 0; }
         capture_version_after() { return 1; }
         run_cmd_bun_with_retry() { log_item "skip" "$1" "dry-run"; return 0; }
@@ -277,20 +281,21 @@ section "Test 3e: legacy cleanup stays non-destructive in dry-run"
 # ============================================================
 dry_run_cleanup_output=$(
     bash -c '
-        source "'"$UPDATE_SH"'"
-
         temp_home="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/acfs-update-dry-run-cleanup.$$"
         mkdir -p "$temp_home/.claude/hooks"
         printf "legacy\n" > "$temp_home/.claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.sh"
 
         HOME="$temp_home"
+        TARGET_HOME="$temp_home"
+        source "'"$UPDATE_SH"'"
+
         DRY_RUN=true
         QUIET=true
         UPDATE_LOG_FILE="/dev/null"
         NO_COLOR=1
         RED="" GREEN="" YELLOW="" CYAN="" BOLD="" DIM="" NC=""
 
-        log_item() { printf "%s|%s|%s\n" "$1" "$2" "$3"; }
+        log_item() { printf "%s|%s|%s\n" "$1" "$2" "${3:-}"; }
 
         cleanup_legacy_git_safety_guard
 
@@ -503,6 +508,19 @@ for ms_arm64_arch in aarch64 arm64; do
                 return 0
             }
 
+            update_binary_path() {
+                if [[ "$1" == "cargo" ]]; then
+                    printf "cargo\n"
+                    return 0
+                fi
+                return 1
+            }
+
+            update_run_in_target_context() {
+                shift
+                "$@"
+            }
+
             update_run_verified_installer ms --easy-mode
         ' 2>&1
     ) || true
